title: Clinical Do Not Rule Schema
description: Schema for a negative clinical rule that specifies what must not or should not be done in a clinical pathway.

source_reference.description: Reference to a source document or source fragment from which the rule was derived.

source_id.description: Stable identifier of the source document in the local knowledge base.

source_level.description: Governance level of the source document.

issuer.description: Organization or authority that issued the source document.

document_title.description: Full title of the source document.

document_version.description: Version, edition, or release identifier of the source document, if available.

publication_date.description: Publication date of the source document, if known.

section_reference.description: Section, chapter, paragraph, or recommendation identifier inside the source document.

page_reference.description: Page or page range inside the source document, if relevant.

statement_quote.description: Short quoted or paraphrased source statement that grounds the rule.

citation_uri.description: Canonical URI, repository URL, or document link for the source, if available.

license_note.description: Any relevant usage, licensing, or reuse note attached to the source content.

actor_role.description: Clinical or organizational role accountable for performing, supporting, or monitoring the rule.

role_name.description: Human-readable name of the role, such as emergency physician, triage nurse, or cath lab team.

responsibility_type.description: Type of responsibility the role has with respect to the rule.

organization_unit.description: Optional service, ward, department, or network node associated with the role.

notes.description: Optional clarifying note about the role or its responsibility boundaries.

criterion.description: Formalized inclusion or exclusion criterion used to determine whether the rule applies.

criterion_id.description: Stable identifier of the criterion inside the rule.

label.description: Short human-readable label for the criterion.

description.description: Clinical meaning of the criterion in plain language.

logic_expression.description: Optional computable or semi-computable expression representing the criterion.

data_elements.description: Identifiers of the data elements needed to test the criterion.

inference_allowed.description: Whether the criterion may be satisfied by inferred evidence from unstructured text or derived signals.

exception_condition.description: Explicit exception, override, or clinically justified deviation condition associated with the rule.

exception_id.description: Stable identifier of the exception inside the rule.

label.description: Short label for the exception or override condition.

description.description: Clinical meaning of the exception in plain language.

condition_logic.description: Optional computable or semi-computable expression that defines the exception.

required_justification.description: Whether explicit justification must be documented when this exception is used.

required_evidence.description: List of evidence items that should support the use of this exception.

trigger.description: Event or condition that makes the rule applicable in a specific patient episode.

trigger_description.description: Human-readable description of what starts applicability of the rule.

anchor_event.description: Canonical event name used as the temporal anchor for downstream constraints, if any.

start_of_applicability.description: Description of when the rule becomes active within the episode.

end_of_applicability.description: Description of when the rule ceases to be applicable.

trigger_data_elements.description: Identifiers of the data elements needed to detect the trigger.

inference_allowed.description: Whether the trigger may be inferred from unstructured notes or derived evidence rather than directly observed structured data.

time_constraint.description: Temporal requirement attached to the rule, relative to an anchor event or timepoint.

constraint_id.description: Stable identifier of the temporal constraint inside the rule.

description.description: Plain-language description of the temporal requirement.

relation_to_anchor.description: Temporal relation between the constrained action and its anchor event.

anchor_event.description: Name of the event or timepoint used as the temporal anchor.

duration_value.description: Numeric duration associated with the constraint, if applicable.

duration_unit.description: Unit of the duration, such as seconds, minutes, hours, or days.

hard_deadline.description: Whether crossing the time limit directly produces non-compliance rather than early warning only.

alert_threshold_value.description: Optional earlier threshold used to raise a weak signal before actual non-compliance.

alert_threshold_unit.description: Unit associated with the alert threshold, if any.

sequence_constraint.description: Ordering requirement between clinical events relevant to the rule.

constraint_id.description: Stable identifier of the sequence constraint inside the rule.

description.description: Plain-language description of the ordering requirement.

predecessor_event.description: Event expected to occur first in the compliant sequence.

successor_event.description: Event expected to occur after the predecessor event.

allowed_order.description: Canonical allowed ordering pattern for the two events.

violation_condition.description: Human-readable or computable condition that defines when the required sequence is broken.

data_element.description: Data element required to evaluate the rule or the weak signal.

data_element_id.description: Stable identifier of the data element in the local data dictionary.

name.description: Human-readable name of the data element.

description.description: Clinical or technical meaning of the data element.

source_location.description: Primary source location from which the data element is expected to be retrieved.

data_type.description: Data type or semantic type of the element, such as datetime, coded concept, numeric, text, or boolean.

coding_system.description: Optional coding system or terminology used by the data element, such as ICD-10, SNOMED CT, LOINC, or local code set.

required_for_evaluation.description: Whether the data element is strictly necessary to evaluate the rule or signal.

extraction_method.description: Method by which the data element is expected to be extracted or derived.

query_hint.description: Optional implementation note, query hint, or extraction hint for engineering teams.

evidence_pattern.description: Pattern of observable evidence used to support compliance, non-compliance, violation, or override states.

evidence_id.description: Stable identifier of the evidence pattern inside the schema instance.

description.description: Plain-language description of the evidence pattern.

evidence_type.description: Type of evidence represented by the pattern.

minimum_occurrences.description: Minimum number of matching observations required for the evidence pattern to be satisfied.

time_reference.description: Optional temporal reference that constrains when the evidence should appear relative to the episode.

derived_from_data_elements.description: Identifiers of the data elements used to instantiate this evidence pattern.

action_parameter.description: Parameter that refines the expected or prohibited action, such as urgency, dosage, route, modality, or threshold.

parameter_description.description: Meaning of the parameter in plain language.

expected_value.description: Expected or prohibited value of the parameter, if known.

comparison_operator.description: Operator used to compare observed data to the expected value, such as equals, less_than, greater_than, or within.

unit.description: Measurement unit associated with the parameter value, if relevant.

mandatory.description: Whether the parameter must be satisfied for the action requirement or prohibition to hold.

completion_criteria.description: Definition of what constitutes completion of the expected action for a do rule.

minimum_occurrences.description: Minimum number of successful action occurrences required to count the rule as fulfilled.

required_event_types.description: Types of events that must be present to claim completion.

required_documentation_elements.description: Documentation artifacts that must exist to support completion, if any.

success_condition.description: Human-readable or semi-formal expression that defines the completion state.

evaluation_logic.description: Logical specification used to decide rule or signal status from observed data.

natural_language_test.description: Plain-language test that a reviewer or engineer can understand without reading source code.

formal_expression_language.description: Language used for the optional computable expression.

formal_expression.description: Optional computable or semi-computable expression implementing the logic.

missing_data_policy.description: Policy to apply when required data are missing.

ambiguity_policy.description: Policy to apply when textual or semantic ambiguity remains after extraction.

conflict_resolution_policy.description: Policy to apply when data sources disagree.

notes.description: Optional implementation notes or caveats about the logical evaluation.

evaluation_method.description: Operational method used to evaluate the rule or detect the weak signal.

allowed_inference_sources.description: Source types that may be used for inference, such as notes, discharge summaries, waveform reports, or derived features.

human_review_required.description: Whether a human reviewer must confirm the final status before operational use.

minimum_confidence.description: Minimum confidence score required for automated acceptance, if the method is inferential or hybrid.

notes.description: Optional notes on model behavior, extraction assumptions, or operational constraints.

risk_classification.description: Risk profile associated with violating the rule or observing the weak signal.

clinical_risk_severity.description: Expected patient-level clinical severity if the rule is violated or the signal materializes.

process_risk_severity.description: Expected process-level severity, including pathway disruption and quality impact.

likelihood_if_violated.description: Estimated likelihood of harm or process failure once the rule is violated.

detectability.description: How easy it is to detect the violation or signal during routine operations.

priority_score.description: Optional local numeric priority score used to rank rules or signals.

rationale.description: Short rationale for the assigned risk classification.

documentation_requirements.description: Documentation requirements attached to the rule.

mandatory_fields.description: Structured or narrative fields that must be documented to support rule evaluation.

acceptable_evidence_sources.description: Accepted evidence sources for documentation purposes, such as EHR forms, notes, orders, procedure logs, or device outputs.

justification_required_when_deviating.description: Whether a documented justification is required when the rule is not followed or an exception is invoked.

justification_fields.description: Fields or artifacts that should contain the deviation or override justification.

free_text_allowed.description: Whether free-text documentation is accepted as valid supporting evidence.

extraction_metadata.description: Metadata about how the schema instance was generated or curated.

generation_mode.description: Method used to create the schema instance.

source_interpretation_notes.description: Notes about interpretation choices, semantic normalization, or unresolved ambiguities in the source text.

extraction_confidence.description: Confidence assigned to the generated schema instance.

ambiguity_flags.description: List of ambiguity flags or warnings that should be reviewed by a human expert.

human_validation_status.description: Current human validation state of the schema instance.

validated_by.description: Person or role that performed the latest human validation, if any.

validated_at.description: Timestamp of the latest human validation, if any.

governance_metadata.description: Lifecycle and governance metadata for operational management of the schema instance.

lifecycle_status.description: Lifecycle state of the rule or weak signal.

version.description: Version identifier of the schema instance.

authoring_body.description: Committee, team, or organization that authored the instance.

rule_owner.description: Operational owner responsible for maintaining the instance over time.

effective_from.description: Date from which the instance is considered effective.

effective_to.description: Date after which the instance is no longer effective, if applicable.

review_cycle.description: Planned review interval or cadence, such as every 12 months.

last_reviewed_at.description: Timestamp of the latest formal review.

approved_by.description: Approver name, role, or committee, if formally approved.

applicability.description: Definition of the patient population and care context in which the rule applies.

population_description.description: Narrative description of the patient population covered by the rule.

clinical_context.description: Clinical situation or disease state in which the rule applies.

care_context.description: Operational or encounter context in which the rule applies, such as pre-hospital, emergency department, ICU, or discharge.

inclusion_criteria.description: Criteria that make a patient episode eligible for the rule.

exclusion_criteria.description: Criteria that make a patient episode ineligible for the rule.

override_justification_requirements.description: Documentation requirements that apply when a do-not rule is intentionally overridden.

mandatory.description: Whether explicit override justification is mandatory.

required_fields.description: Fields or artifacts that must capture the override justification.

required_evidence_sources.description: Evidence sources that may legitimately support the override.

notes.description: Optional note about acceptable override scenarios or reviewer expectations.

triggering_pattern.description: Observable pattern that defines the weak signal.

pattern_type.description: Canonical category of the weak signal pattern.

pattern_description.description: Human-readable description of the pattern to be detected.

anchor_event.description: Optional reference event used as the temporal anchor for pattern detection.

target_event.description: Event whose absence, presence, or timing contributes to the weak signal.

supporting_context.description: Additional context needed to interpret the pattern correctly.

inference_allowed.description: Whether the pattern may rely on inferred evidence from unstructured text or derived variables.

precursor_window.description: Time window within which the weak signal is meaningful relative to rule applicability or process anchors.

start_anchor.description: Anchor event that defines the beginning of the precursor window.

start_offset_value.description: Numeric offset from the start anchor, if applicable.

start_offset_unit.description: Unit of the start offset, if applicable.

end_anchor.description: Anchor event that defines the end of the precursor window.

end_offset_value.description: Numeric offset from the end anchor, if applicable.

end_offset_unit.description: Unit of the end offset, if applicable.

description.description: Plain-language description of the precursor window.

signal_threshold.description: Threshold or boundary used to convert raw observations into a weak signal state.

threshold_description.description: Meaning of the threshold in plain language.

comparison_operator.description: Operator used to compare observed data to the threshold value.

threshold_value.description: Threshold value, which may be numeric or categorical.

unit.description: Measurement unit for the threshold, if relevant.

severity_when_crossed.description: Severity assigned when the threshold is crossed.

risk_implication.description: Expected downstream impact of a weak signal on rule non-compliance or pathway safety.

expected_non_compliance_type.description: Most likely category of downstream non-compliance indicated by the weak signal.

contribution_severity.description: Severity of the signal's contribution to downstream risk.

likelihood_contribution.description: Strength of the association between the signal and downstream non-compliance.

expected_time_to_failure.description: Estimated time horizon within which non-compliance may occur after the signal appears.

escalation_priority.description: Operational urgency with which the signal should be escalated.

explanation.description: Short rationale explaining why the signal implies downstream risk.

recommended_response.description: Recommended operational response when the weak signal is detected.

immediate_action.description: Action to perform immediately when the signal is detected.

follow_up_action.description: Subsequent action to perform after the immediate response, if any.

responsible_role.description: Role expected to own the response.

max_response_time_value.description: Maximum allowed response time before escalation, if applicable.

max_response_time_unit.description: Unit of the maximum response time, if applicable.

requires_human_review.description: Whether a human review step is mandatory before acting on the signal.

validation_requirements.description: Requirements for confirming that the weak signal is valid and operationally trustworthy.

must_be_reviewed_by_human.description: Whether the signal requires human confirmation before being used operationally.

minimum_supporting_evidence_count.description: Minimum number of supporting evidence items required for acceptance.

acceptable_confirmation_sources.description: Sources that may confirm the signal, such as notes, telemetry, order logs, or procedure records.

retrospective_validation_possible.description: Whether the signal can be validated reliably in retrospective review.

derivation_metadata.description: Metadata describing how the weak signal was derived from the parent rule and process-risk analysis.

derived_from_rule_id.description: Identifier of the parent rule from which the weak signal was derived.

derivation_method.description: Method used to derive the weak signal.

derivation_rationale.description: Narrative explanation of why this signal is considered an early indicator of downstream risk.

derivation_confidence.description: Confidence assigned to the derivation of the weak signal.

supporting_rule_fragments.description: Relevant fragments, conditions, or elements of the parent rule that support this weak signal.

schema_version.description: Version of this JSON schema used to encode the rule instance.

rule_id.description: Stable unique identifier of the rule.

rule_type.description: Fixed discriminator indicating that this is a do-not rule.

title.description: Full human-readable title of the rule.

short_label.description: Optional short label for dashboards, tables, or user interfaces.

clinical_pathway_id.description: Identifier of the clinical pathway or PDTA to which the rule belongs.

clinical_pathway_name.description: Human-readable name of the clinical pathway or PDTA.

clinical_phase.description: Phase of the pathway in which the rule applies, such as pre-hospital, triage, diagnosis, reperfusion, monitoring, or discharge.

care_settings.description: Care settings in which the rule may apply.

responsible_actors.description: Roles responsible for avoiding, preventing, or reviewing the prohibited action.

deontic_strength.description: Normative strength of the prohibition after semantic normalization.

recommendation_strength.description: Original or mapped strength-of-recommendation label, if available from the source.

evidence_level.description: Original or mapped evidence level attached to the rule, if available from the source.

original_statement.description: Original or normalized source statement from which the rule was derived.

normalized_statement.description: Single-sentence normalized expression of the prohibition suitable for downstream computation.

intent.description: Short statement of the clinical and operational intent of the prohibition.

rationale.description: Optional explanation of why the prohibited action is unsafe, unnecessary, or process-distorting.

source_references.description: Source references that ground the rule.

exceptions.description: Explicit exceptions that suspend the prohibition under defined clinical conditions.

prohibited_action.description: Action that must not or should not be performed while the rule is active.

action_verb.description: Canonical action verb, such as perform, request, administer, delay, repeat, or transfer.

action_description.description: Full plain-language description of the prohibited action.

action_target.description: Clinical object, observation, order, procedure, medication, or workflow item targeted by the prohibited action.

known_risk_if_performed.description: Expected clinical or process harm if the prohibited action is performed.

override_conditions.description: Conditions under which the prohibited action may be intentionally overridden.

time_constraints.description: Temporal constraints that define when the prohibition starts, ends, or is especially critical.

sequence_constraints.description: Ordering constraints that help determine whether a prohibited action occurred in an unacceptable sequence.

required_data_elements.description: Data elements required to determine applicability, detect commission of the prohibited action, and assess overrides.

evidence_of_violation.description: Patterns of evidence that support the conclusion that the prohibited action occurred.

evidence_of_allowed_override.description: Patterns of evidence that support an allowed override of the prohibition.

possible_outcomes.description: Evaluation outcomes that are valid for this rule in operational use.

associated_weak_signal_ids.description: Identifiers of weak signals that are explicitly associated with this rule.
